content.text: Agents store conversation history, tool registries, retrieved documents, and execution traces in Python collections. Choosing the right structure — list, tuple, set, or dict — affects ordering guarantees, lookup speed, deduplication, and whether state can be accidentally mutated across steps.
Collections are container types that hold multiple values. The four core built-ins for agent code are:
— unordered collection of unique hashable items
— mapping from hashable keys to arbitrary values
for O(1) membership. Fixed routing keys may use
. Wrong structure choice leads to duplicate tool calls, lost ordering, or unhashable-key errors.
— preserve order for conversation turns and plan steps
— fast key lookup for configs, tool results keyed by call ID
— deduplicate URLs, document IDs, visited nodes in search agents
— lightweight immutable records; hashable when all elements are hashable
— O(n) membership test; use set when checking many times
— keys must be hashable (no lists as keys); insertion-ordered since Python 3.7
— shared references to lists/dicts mutate across aliases

history = []
history.append({"role": "user", "content": "Hi"})
history.append({"role": "assistant", "content": "Hello"})

last = history[-1]
history.extend(more_turns)  # add multiple
history.pop()                 # remove last

route = ("planner", "executor")  # fixed pipeline pair
lat_lon = (40.7, -74.0)

# unpacking
model, fallback = ("gpt-4o", "gpt-4o-mini")
— ordered sequences: messages, steps, batch jobs
— labeled fields: tool args, config, JSON-like objects
— uniqueness and fast membership: permissions, visited IDs
— fixed-size records, dict keys, return multiple values

# Nested structures — common in agent traces
trace = {
    "run_id": "abc",
    "steps": [
        {"tool": "search", "args": {"q": "python"}, "ms": 120},
        {"tool": "summarize", "args": {"text": "..."}, "ms": 890},
    ],
}

# Copy vs. alias
import copy
shallow = trace.copy()           # top-level dict copy
deep = copy.deepcopy(trace)      # full recursive copy
A retrieval agent deduplicates chunk IDs while preserving ranked order in a list:
Using a list for membership checks
in hot paths — convert to set once
fails; use tuples or string keys
in older mental models — sets are unordered; sort explicitly when needed
for message lists (details in Type Hints topic)
Treat shared collections as owned by one layer; pass copies across agent boundaries when isolation matters
Prefer dict comprehensions (Comprehensions topic) for transforming tool output maps

Dict and set average-case lookup is O(1). List append is amortized O(1); insert at front is O(n). For large conversation windows, truncate or summarize rather than letting lists grow unbounded in memory.
(OOP topic) improve clarity over plain dicts. For ordered unique items,
(Python 3.7+) preserves insertion order unlike set iteration sorting.
. Pick the container that matches how you look up, order, and mutate data.
Messages and steps → lists; configs and results → dicts; allowlists → sets; fixed pairs → tuples
Understand alias vs. copy for mutable nested state in agents
Membership-heavy logic belongs in sets, not lists
Nested JSON-like structures are idiomatic but need careful copying
